perturbation.original_text: All Confidential Information provided by HPAC and any copies made thereof shall remain the sole property of HPAC and shall be returned or destroyed, at the option of HPAC, upon three (3) days' written notice to LHT. Return or destruction of the Confidential Information and any copies made thereof shall not relieve LHT of its obligations hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: All Confidential Information provided by HPAC and any copies made thereof shall remain the sole property of HPAC and shall be returned or destroyed, at the option of LHT, upon three (3) days' written notice to HPAC. Return or destruction of the Confidential Information and any copies made thereof shall not relieve LHT of its obligations hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the option to return or destroy to LHT instead of HPAC, this creates a direct contradiction. The original text gives HPAC control over the Confidential Information's destiny. The modified text gives that control to LHT, making it unclear who ultimately decides what happens to the information. This new text is in conflict within the same clause, since the information remains the sole property of HPAC.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Nothing herein shall be construed to obligate HPAC to disclose any Confidential Information to LHT, or as granting a license to any Confidential Information disclosed. HPAC makes no representation or warranty regarding the completeness or accuracy of any Confidential Information disclosed. All Confidential Information provided by HPAC and any copies made thereof shall remain the sole property of HPAC and shall be returned or destroyed, at the option of LHT, upon three (3) days' written notice to HPAC. Return or destruction of the Confidential Information and any copies made thereof shall not relieve LHT of its obligations hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The original text gives HPAC the option to return or destroy Confidential Information, maintaining control as the owner. The changed text gives this option to LHT, directly contradicting the statement that HPAC retains sole property. The contradicted text repeats the modified text, further solidifying the in-text contradiction. This change creates a conflict in the clause itself, as it says the information remains HPAC's property, but LHT has the authority to destroy it. This contradicts the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The above restrictions shall not apply to Confidential Information which (a) is in the public domain or is properly in the possession of the LHT without restriction at the time of receipt hereunder; (b) subsequently enters the public domain without breach hereof by LHT; (c) is lawfully received from a third party without restriction on disclosure; (d) is independently developed by LHT's personnel who have not had direct or indirect access to or knowledge of the Confidential Information; (e) is used or disclosed with the prior written approval of HPAC; and (f) is obligated to be produced under order of a court of competent jurisdiction (provided that the LHT gives adequate notice of such pending order to HPAC so that HPAC can seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy).
perturbation.changed_text: The above restrictions shall not apply to Confidential Information which (a) is in the public domain or is properly in the possession of the LHT without restriction at the time of receipt hereunder; (b) subsequently enters the public domain without breach hereof by LHT; (c) is lawfully received from a third party without restriction on disclosure; (d) is independently developed by LHT's personnel who have not had direct or indirect access to or knowledge of the Confidential Information; (e) is used or disclosed with the prior written approval of HPAC.
perturbation.explanation: By removing exception (f), which deals with court-ordered disclosure, we create a conflict. The original text acknowledges that disclosure may be required by court order and provides a process for it. Removing this exception now implies that LHT is not allowed to disclose Confidential Information under any circumstances, even if legally compelled to do so. This contradicts the legal reality that court orders must be obeyed. The change contradict the possibility that even if it is confidential information, it needs to be disclosed in special cases.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The above restrictions shall not apply to Confidential Information which (a) is in the public domain or is properly in the possession of the LHT without restriction at the time of receipt hereunder; (b) subsequently enters the public domain without breach hereof by LHT; (c) is lawfully received from a third party without restriction on disclosure; (d) is independently developed by LHT's personnel who have not had direct or indirect access to or knowledge of the Confidential Information; (e) is used or disclosed with the prior written approval of HPAC. If the above exclusions apply to only a portion of the Confidential Information, then only that portion shall be excluded from the restrictions contained herein.
perturbation.justification: The original text includes an exception (f) that allows for court-ordered disclosure of confidential information, outlining a procedure for such disclosure. The changed text removes this exception. Consequently, the changed text implies that LHT cannot disclose Confidential Information under any circumstances. The contradicted text, which is identical to the changed text, also lacks this exception, solidifying the implication that no court-ordered disclosure is permitted. This creates a direct conflict because, in reality, court orders must be obeyed, and the contract, as modified, now contradicts this legal reality by seemingly prohibiting such legally mandated disclosure. The removal of the exception (f) from the original text to the changed text causes the contradiction with the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: LHT agrees to comply with all governmental regulations applicable to the Confidential Information, including federal and state securities laws and US Export Control Laws and Regulations.
perturbation.changed_text: LHT agrees to comply with some governmental regulations applicable to the Confidential Information, including federal and state securities laws and US Export Control Laws and Regulations.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "all" to "some" introduces uncertainty and contradiction. The original text states a clear obligation for LHT to comply with *all* applicable governmental regulations. The modified text creates ambiguity as to which regulations LHT *must* comply with. The new wording is contradictory since it initially indicates compliance with some laws, but not all. There is no distinction of which ones LHT needs to comply with, or not, opening a contradiction of enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall not be assigned by either party without the express written consent of the other party; however, either party may assign this Agreement to its respective successors in interest. This Agreement constitutes the entire understanding between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof, and may be modified only by a written amendment signed by both parties. LHT agrees to comply with some governmental regulations applicable to the Confidential Information, including federal and state securities laws and US Export Control Laws and Regulations.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'all' to 'some' in the Changed Text directly contradicts the intent of the original text and introduces ambiguity. The Contradicted Text includes the modified clause within the agreement. The original clause mandated compliance with all governmental regulations, while the modified clause introduces uncertainty about which regulations LHT is obligated to comply with. This uncertainty leads to a contradiction, as it's unclear whether LHT must comply with all regulations as previously stated or only a subset of them. It is a structural flaw that introduces an in-text contradiction, and makes enforcement unclear and inconsistent. The Perturbation Explanation also confirms this, stating that the new wording creates ambiguity, and opening a contradiction of enforcement.
